title: Apply automotive mechanical and electrical knowledge to vehicle loss assessments
training_package_name: Automotive Retail, Service and Repair Training Package
competent_person_statement: A competent person in Apply automotive mechanical and electrical knowledge to vehicle loss assessments demonstrates consistent performance across all elements and performance criteria in a workplace context.

elements.title: Develop and apply an understanding of automotive mechanical knowledge

pcs.text: Employ research techniques, including discussions with customers and supervisors, and advanced specialist knowledge to identify vehicle mechanical damage 1.2 Develop knowledge of the operating principles of suspension and steering systems, brake systems, transmission and driveline assembly, and engine and fuel systems1.3 Apply knowledge of suspension and steering systems, brake systems, transmission and driveline assembly, and engine and fuel systems to loss assessment processes, procedures and policies
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Employ research techniques, including discussions with customers and supervisors, and advanced specialist knowledge to identify vehicle mechanical damage 1.2 Develop knowledge of the operating principles of suspension and steering systems, brake systems, transmission and driveline assembly, and engine and fuel systems1.3 Apply knowledge of suspension and steering systems, brake systems, transmission and driveline assembly, and engine and fuel systems to loss assessment processes, procedures and policies?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Employ research techniques, including discussions with customers and supervisors, and advanced specialist knowledge to identify vehicle mechanical damage 1.2 Develop knowledge of the operating principles of suspension and steering systems, brake systems, transmission and driveline assembly, and engine and fuel systems1.3 Apply knowledge of suspension and steering systems, brake systems, transmission and driveline assembly, and engine and fuel systems to loss assessment processes, procedures and policies.
